title: CLAUDE.md
text: ebrdify — R (Stata) package classifies countries dataset EBRD groupings standardises country names official EBRD / Transition Report (TR) terminology.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Given column vector country identifiers (ISO3, ISO2, country names), package tags economy EBRD classification can rewrite name official form used EBRD Transition Report. Public API: ebrdify(data, var, var_format) — appends classification columns: ebrd (EBRD country operation), coo_group (traditional regional grouping), eu_ebrd (EBRD economy also EU member), coo_group_alt (alternative grouping), ebrd_shareholder. list_ebrd(, group) — returns full list EBRD economies ISO3 codes, official names, ; optionally filtered one coo_group. canonise(x, ) — rewrites country identifiers official EBRD/TR names (e.g. Czech Republic → Czechia, Palestine → West Bank Gaza, Kyrgyzstan → Kyrgyz Republic, Taiwan → Taipei China).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: classification naming data come Annex OCE TR style guide (~/Documents/GitHub/tr-style-guide, section #sec-annex-oce_style_guide_v1.qmd, plus groupings/comparators.md). guide changes, update R/lookups.R — single -package source truth every lookup table derived. Keep Stata implementation (stata/ebrdify.ado) sync manually.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: EBRD economies use official names .ebrd_economies (Annex , tbl-ebrd-coo). “Forbidden names” never used: Türkiye (Turkey), Kyrgyz Republic (Kyrgyzstan), Slovak Republic (Slovakia), Côte d’Ivoire (Ivory Coast), West Bank Gaza (Palestine), Czechia (Czech Republic). non-EBRD comparators also required names: Taipei China (Taiwan), Hong Kong SAR, Macao SAR. live .tr_name_override. canonise() applies overrides top countrycode’s English short names, countrycode returns wrong form several (e.g. gives “Turkey”, “Kyrgyzstan”, “Slovakia”, “Palestinian Territories”, “Taiwan”).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Russia, Belarus Cyprus EBRD economies must never appear EBRD groupings (Russia/Belarus/Cyprus appear shareholder list). Czechia Greece removed EBRD countries operation 2026 (NEWS 0.4.3–0.4.4). longer grouping, Czechia/Greece remain valid comparator names remain EBRD shareholders. Kosovo: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code XKX, EBRD also uses KOS. Internally everything normalised XKX (see .to_iso3c()), either input code classifies correctly. 41 EBRD economies. Türkiye region — part traditional grouping. shareholder list intentionally includes non-country entities (EIB, EUU) non-COO shareholders; broader 41 economies.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/lookups.R — internal data. master .ebrd_economies data frame named-vector lookups derived . Built namespace loads, per call — main performance lever. R/utils.R — internal helpers: .clean_input(), .detect_format(), .to_iso3c() (shared format detection + ISO3 conversion). R/ebrdify.R — ebrdify(). R/list_ebrd.R — list_ebrd(). R/canonise.R — canonise(). ebrdify() canonise() route identifier → ISO3 .to_iso3c(), calls countrycode unique non-ISO3 values (expensive step) uses O(1) named-vector lookups everything else.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tests live tests/testthat/; tests/testthat.R runs test_check("ebrdify"). Bump Version DESCRIPTION add NEWS.md entry classification/naming change. NEWS tracks R implementation . Non-ASCII names written \u escapes (e.g. "Türkiye", "Côte d'Ivoire") package stays portable R CMD check-clean. dev/, stata/, data-raw/, pkgdown/ build-ignored (.Rbuildignore).
code: devtools::load_all()        # load during development devtools::document()        # regenerate NAMESPACE + man/ after changing roxygen devtools::test()            # run testthat suite (tests/testthat/) devtools::check()           # full R CMD check Rscript dev/benchmark.r     # compare against dev/ebrdify_old.R

what: A handy function to make your data EBRD-friendly
title: A handy function to make your data EBRD-friendly
text: handy function classify EBRD countries operation

title: A handy function to make your data EBRD-friendly
A handy function to make your data EBRD-friendly
text: Country names groupings follow Annex EBRD OCE Transition Report style guide.
code: library(ebrdify)  # Classify the countries in your data into EBRD groupings. # Accepts ISO3, ISO2 or country names (format auto-detected if not given). df <- data.frame(country = c("Kazakhstan", "Poland", "Germany", "Nigeria")) ebrdify(df, "country") #>      country ebrd                        coo_group eu_ebrd ...  # Get the full list of EBRD economies (ISO3 codes, official names, or both). list_ebrd()                            # data frame of all 41 economies list_ebrd("iso3c")                     # just the ISO3 codes list_ebrd("name", group = "Central Asia")  # Rewrite country names to official EBRD / Transition Report terminology. canonise(c("Czech Republic", "Palestine", "Kyrgyzstan", "Taiwan")) #> "Czechia" "West Bank and Gaza" "Kyrgyz Republic" "Taipei China"

title: A handy function to make your data EBRD-friendly
text: ⚠️ Warning: changelog version history repository track changes R implementation. Stata version updates, please check Stata folder directly.

what: Canonise country names to official EBRD terminology — canonise
title: Canonise country names to official EBRD terminology — canonise
text: Rewrites country identifiers (names, ISO3 ISO2 codes) official country names used EBRD Transition Report. enforces TR house style "forbidden names" rules Annex style guide — example:

title: Canonise country names to official EBRD terminology — canonise
Canonise country names to official EBRD terminology — canonise
text: x vector country identifiers (names, ISO3, ISO2 codes). Format x: "country.name", "iso3c", "iso2c". NULL (default) format auto-detected. warn TRUE (default), report identifiers matched via message().

title: Canonise country names to official EBRD terminology — canonise
text: character vector length x official EBRD/TR names, NA identifiers matched. Designed drop dplyr::mutate(), e.g. mutate(country = canonise(country)).

title: Canonise country names to official EBRD terminology — canonise
text: Czech Republic \(\rightarrow\) Czechia Palestine \(\rightarrow\) West Bank Gaza Kyrgyzstan \(\rightarrow\) Kyrgyz Republic Slovakia \(\rightarrow\) Slovak Republic Turkey \(\rightarrow\) Türkiye Ivory Coast \(\rightarrow\) Côte d'Ivoire Taiwan \(\rightarrow\) Taipei China EBRD economies named official Annex list; economies fall back countrycode's English short name, TR-specific overrides applied top (Taipei China, Hong Kong SAR, Macao SAR, Czechia) countrycode returns non-TR form .

title: Canonise country names to official EBRD terminology — canonise
code: canonise(c("Czech Republic", "Palestine", "Kyrgyzstan", "Taiwan")) #> [1] "Czechia"            "West Bank and Gaza" "Kyrgyz Republic"    #> [4] "Taipei China"       canonise(c("TUR", "SVK", "CIV"), from = "iso3c") #> [1] "Türkiye"         "Slovak Republic" "Côte d'Ivoire"   canonise(c("Germany", "Wakanda"))   # non-EBRD pass through; unmatched -> NA #> The following entries could not be matched: Wakanda #> [1] "Germany" NA

what: Classify countries into EBRD groupings — ebrdify
title: Classify countries into EBRD groupings — ebrdify
text: Tags country dataset EBRD status regional groupings, following official EBRD / Transition Report classification (Annex OCE TR style guide). Country identifiers may ISO3 codes, ISO2 codes, country names, format auto-detected supplied.

title: Classify countries into EBRD groupings — ebrdify
Classify countries into EBRD groupings — ebrdify
text: data data frame containing country variable, NULL passing vector via var. var Either name column data holding country identifiers, — data NULL — vector country identifiers. var_format Format identifiers: "country.name", "iso3c", "iso2c". NULL (default) format auto-detected.

title: Classify countries into EBRD groupings — ebrdify
text: data frame five appended columns. data supplied original columns kept added; otherwise data frame just columns (one row per input element) returned. ebrd 1 EBRD country operation, 0 otherwise, NA identifier matched. coo_group Traditional EBRD regional grouping, NA. eu_ebrd 1 EBRD economy EU member, else 0 (NA unmatched). coo_group_alt Alternative EBRD grouping, NA. ebrd_shareholder 1 EBRD shareholder, else 0 (NA unmatched). Unmatched identifiers reported via message().

title: Classify countries into EBRD groupings — ebrdify
code: # Using a data frame df <- data.frame(country_code = c("KAZ", "HRV", "NGA", "ARM", "USA")) ebrdify(df, "country_code", var_format = "iso3c") #>   country_code ebrd                        coo_group eu_ebrd #> 1          KAZ    1                     Central Asia       0 #> 2          HRV    1 Central Europe and Baltic States       1 #> 3          NGA    1               Sub-Saharan Africa       0 #> 4          ARM    1  Eastern Europe and the Caucasus       0 #> 5          USA    0                             <NA>       0 #>                    coo_group_alt ebrd_shareholder #> 1 Former Soviet Union + Mongolia                1 #> 2                        EU-EBRD                1 #> 3             Sub-Saharan Africa                1 #> 4 Former Soviet Union + Mongolia                1 #> 5                           <NA>                1  # Using a vector, with auto-detected format ebrdify(var = c("Kazakhstan", "Croatia", "Narnia", "United States")) #> The following entries could not be matched: Narnia #>   ebrd                        coo_group eu_ebrd                  coo_group_alt #> 1    1                     Central Asia       0 Former Soviet Union + Mongolia #> 2    1 Central Europe and Baltic States       1                        EU-EBRD #> 3   NA                             <NA>      NA                           <NA> #> 4    0                             <NA>       0                           <NA> #>   ebrd_shareholder #> 1                1 #> 2                1 #> 3               NA #> 4                1

title: List EBRD economies — list_ebrd
text: Returns full list 41 EBRD economies — ISO3 codes, official EBRD/Transition Report names, — optionally restricted single traditional regional grouping.

title: List EBRD economies — list_ebrd
text: return: "" (default) data frame iso3c name columns; "iso3c" character vector ISO3 codes; "name" character vector official names. group Optional. One traditional coo_group filter , e.g. "Central Asia". NULL (default) returns economies. See list_ebrd_groups() valid values.

title: List EBRD economies — list_ebrd
text: data frame (= "") character vector, ordered alphabetically official name (code = "iso3c").

title: List EBRD economies — list_ebrd
code: list_ebrd()                       # data frame of all 41 economies #>    iso3c                   name #> 1    ALB                Albania #> 2    ARM                Armenia #> 3    AZE             Azerbaijan #> 4    BEN                  Benin #> 5    BIH Bosnia and Herzegovina #> 6    BGR               Bulgaria #> 7    HRV                Croatia #> 8    CIV          Côte d'Ivoire #> 9    EGY                  Egypt #> 10   EST                Estonia #> 11   GEO                Georgia #> 12   GHA                  Ghana #> 13   HUN                Hungary #> 14   IRQ                   Iraq #> 15   JOR                 Jordan #> 16   KAZ             Kazakhstan #> 17   KEN                  Kenya #> 18   XKX                 Kosovo #> 19   KGZ        Kyrgyz Republic #> 20   LVA                 Latvia #> 21   LBN                Lebanon #> 22   LTU              Lithuania #> 23   MDA                Moldova #> 24   MNG               Mongolia #> 25   MNE             Montenegro #> 26   MAR                Morocco #> 27   NGA                Nigeria #> 28   MKD        North Macedonia #> 29   POL                 Poland #> 30   ROU                Romania #> 31   SEN                Senegal #> 32   SRB                 Serbia #> 33   SVK        Slovak Republic #> 34   SVN               Slovenia #> 35   TJK             Tajikistan #> 36   TUN                Tunisia #> 37   TKM           Turkmenistan #> 38   TUR                Türkiye #> 39   UKR                Ukraine #> 40   UZB             Uzbekistan #> 41   PSE     West Bank and Gaza list_ebrd("iso3c")                # just the ISO3 codes #>  [1] "ALB" "ARM" "AZE" "BEN" "BGR" "BIH" "CIV" "EGY" "EST" "GEO" "GHA" "HRV" #> [13] "HUN" "IRQ" "JOR" "KAZ" "KEN" "KGZ" "LBN" "LTU" "LVA" "MAR" "MDA" "MKD" #> [25] "MNE" "MNG" "NGA" "POL" "PSE" "ROU" "SEN" "SRB" "SVK" "SVN" "TJK" "TKM" #> [37] "TUN" "TUR" "UKR" "UZB" "XKX" list_ebrd("name", group = "Central Asia") #> [1] "Kazakhstan"      "Kyrgyz Republic" "Mongolia"        "Tajikistan"      #> [5] "Turkmenistan"    "Uzbekistan"

title: EBRD regional groupings — list_ebrd_groups
text: Returns names traditional EBRD regional groupings (coo_group) can passed group argument list_ebrd().

title: EBRD regional groupings — list_ebrd_groups
text: character vector grouping names.

title: EBRD regional groupings — list_ebrd_groups
code: list_ebrd_groups() #> [1] "Central Asia"                       "Central Europe and Baltic States"   #> [3] "Eastern Europe and the Caucasus"    "South-eastern Europe"               #> [5] "Southern and Eastern Mediterranean" "Sub-Saharan Africa"                 #> [7] "Türkiye"

title: ebrdify 0.5.0
text: New list_ebrd() returns full list EBRD economies ISO3 codes, official names, , optionally filtered one regional grouping; list_ebrd_groups() lists available groupings. New canonise() rewrites country identifiers official EBRD / Transition Report names (e.g. Czech Republic → Czechia, Palestine → West Bank Gaza, Kyrgyzstan → Kyrgyz Republic, Taiwan → Taipei China). Fixed crash ebrdify() (“row names contain missing values”) occurred common inputs mixing EBRD non-EBRD countries (e.g. vector like c("KAZ", "USA")). ebrdify() now correctly classifies Kosovo whether passed XKX KOS. Performance: classification lookup tables now built package loads instead every call, hot path avoids redundant work. Dropped dplyr tidyr dependencies (unused), lighter, faster install load. Added real test suite (tests/testthat.R previously empty, R CMD check ran tests).
